summary: Cleanup pass centered on src/melee/ft/fighter.c and fighter.h. The PR propagated more semantic fighter/action-state naming across C, headers, variables comments, and asm labels: FtState became ActionState, x10 became x10_action_state_index, x890 became x890_cameraBox, selected ftCommonData fields gained purpose hints, and func_80068E64/func_8006A1BC were renamed into Fighter_* symbols. The largest code changes are readability-only refactors in fighter spawn and action-state-change code, replacing generic temporaries such as temp_r31/fp/arg1 with fighterObj/fighter/new_action_state_index and updating call sites accordingly. No review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Renamed the action-state data structures and globals from FtState/FtStateList/FtStateTableByCharacter to ActionState/ActionStateList/ActionStateTableByCharacter in fighter.c, fighter.h, asm/melee/ft/ftdata.s, and include/variables.h.
Renamed Fighter struct field x10 to x10_action_state_index and updated references in fighter.c, ftlib.c, ftMarsSpecialS.c, ftclink.c, ftdrmario.c, and other fighter code touched by the diff.
Renamed Fighter struct fields x1C_ftStateList and x20_ftStateList to x1C_actionStateList and x20_actionStateList to match the ActionState terminology.
Renamed Fighter struct field x890 to x890_cameraBox and updated camera-box uses in fighter.c, ftlib.c, and ftcliffcommon.c.
Renamed ftCommonData fields with observed semantics: x6F4 to x6F4_unkDamage, x7B4 to x7B4_unkDamage, and x7E4 to x7E4_scaleZ.
Renamed func_80068E64 to Fighter_80068E64 and updated the asm call in code_800877F8.s plus declarations in include/functions.h and fighter.h.
Renamed func_8006A1BC to Fighter_8006A1BC and updated the process registration in func_80068E98.
Adjusted func_8006A360's prototype from taking an unused second argument to HSD_GObj* only, preserving an unused local in the function body.
Cleaned up func_80068E98 variable names: temp_r31 became fighterObj, temp_r28_2 became jobj, vec/vec2 became translation/translation2, and several Fighter* temporaries became fp/fp1/fp2.
Cleaned up Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC argument and local names: arg1 became new_action_state_index, fp became fighter, and ftState became new_action_state.
Removed some decomp.me scratch/matched comments and simplified small formatting such as the g_spawnNumCounter wraparound check.
Added a local HALF_PI define in fighter.c, though the excerpt does not show its use.
smart_moves: The PR kept asm symbols, C externs, header prototypes, and comments in include/variables.h synchronized with renamed globals, reducing the chance of linker or declaration drift.

Renaming x10 to x10_action_state_index captured a high-value semantic field that appears broadly in fighter and character-special logic, making future action-state code much easier to search and audit.
The ActionState rename avoids the ambiguity of FtState, which could be confused with the full Fighter object state rather than action-state table entries.
CameraBox naming on x890 clarified pointer ownership and made downstream ftlib/ftcliffcommon call sites self-documenting without changing APIs.
Field suffixes such as _scaleZ and _unkDamage encode partial confidence: they preserve offset identity while adding observed use semantics.
The spawn function cleanup reused existing calls and order while improving variable names, a low-risk way to make a large matched function more maintainable.
decomp_lessons: When a struct field's role is clear from multiple call sites, rename the field and update all character-specific users in the same PR to prevent mixed old/new terminology.
For global data emitted from asm, rename both the .global label and every C extern/declaration that references it; otherwise cleanup can silently create symbol mismatch risk.
Prefer semantic argument names on central functions like Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC. Naming arg1 as new_action_state_index documents the contract for every caller.
Partial names are acceptable when evidence is incomplete: x6F4_unkDamage and x7B4_unkDamage are better than raw offsets but still signal uncertainty.
Avoid leaving decomp.me scratch comments as permanent documentation once a function is integrated; useful knowledge should become symbol, field, and local names.

Renaming locals in matched functions can be valuable even without behavioral changes, especially when temporaries represent persistent concepts like fighterObj, jobj, translation, or new_action_state.
naming_conventions: Use ActionState for action-state table structs and globals rather than FtState.
Name action-state index fields with the suffix _action_state_index, as in x10_action_state_index.
Name Fighter process/helper functions with a Fighter_ prefix when promoting from anonymous func_ symbols, as in Fighter_80068E64 and Fighter_8006A1BC.
Preserve offset prefixes on partially understood struct fields while appending semantic hints, such as x890_cameraBox, x7E4_scaleZ, x6F4_unkDamage, and x7B4_unkDamage.
Use descriptive local names for object lifetimes: fighterObj for HSD_GObj*, fighter/fp for Fighter*, jobj for HSD_JObj*, and translation/translation2 for Vec values.
Use new_action_state_index and new_action_state in action-state transition code to distinguish the target state from current state fields.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed asm labels in ftdata.s from FtStateList/FtStateTableByCharacter to ActionStateList/ActionStateTableByCharacter while preserving table layout and entries.
Updated an asm branch/call comment target in code_800877F8.s from func_80068E64 to Fighter_80068E64 without changing the instruction bytes.
Kept behavior stable while improving C readability by renaming locals and replacing temporary variables with nested calls where the same call order could be preserved.
Function prototype cleanup for func_8006A360 removed an unused external parameter and introduced an unused local, suggesting a matching-conscious adjustment rather than a behavior change.
The diff keeps offset-based field names in place while adding suffix semantics, which helps matching work continue even before full struct recovery.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
Because there was no review discussion, inferred intent is limited to the title and diff: a Fighter.c cleanup pass focused on naming and readability rather than new functionality.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that build on ActionStateList or ActionStateTableByCharacter naming.
Check whether x6F4_unkDamage and x7B4_unkDamage received more precise names in later fighter cleanup commits.
Search for remaining raw Fighter.x10 uses after PR #360 to identify missed action-state-index call sites.
Search for remaining x890 uses to confirm x890_cameraBox propagation was completed across the tree.
Inspect later reviews touching Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC for naming refinements to arg2 flags or ActionState fields.
